When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed.\n   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues.\n   - When CI is failing, make sure you download the logs locally and carefully investigate them.\n   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.\n   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.\n   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an ./examples and/or ./experiments folders so you can reuse them later.\n   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to ./experiments folder.\n   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to ./examples folder.\n   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.\nInitial research.\n   - When you start, make sure you create detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step, make sure that as many points from these guidelines are added to your todo list to keep track of everything that can help you solve the issue with highest possible quality.\n   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1246.\n   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner link-assistant [keywords].\n   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.\n   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.\n   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.\n   - When accessing GitHub Gists, use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching.\n   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.\n   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.\n   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has THREE different comment types with different API endpoints:\n      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/pulls/1247/comments --paginate\n      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1247/comments --paginate\n      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/pulls/1247/reviews --paginate\n      IMPORTANT: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" ONLY returns conversation comments and misses review comments!\n   - When you need latest comments on issue, use gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1246/comments --paginate.\nSolution development and testing.\n   - When issue is solvable, implement code with tests.\n   - When coding, each atomic step that can be useful by itself should be commited to the pull request's branch, meaning if work will be interrupted by any reason parts of solution will still be kept intact and safe in pull request.\n   - When you test:\n      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;\n      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.\n   - When you test integrations, use existing framework.\n   - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.\n   - When issue is unclear, write comment on issue asking questions.\n   - When you encounter any problems that you unable to solve yourself, write a comment to the pull request asking for help.\n   - When you need human help, use gh pr comment 1247 --body \"your message\" to comment on existing PR.\nPreparing pull request.\n   - When you code, follow contributing guidelines.\n   - When you commit, write clear message.\n   - When you need examples of style, use gh pr list --repo link-assistant/hive-mind --state merged --search [keywords].\n   - When you open pr, describe solution draft and include tests.\n   - When there is a package with version and GitHub Actions workflows for automatic release, update the version in your pull request to prepare for next release.\n   - When you update existing pr 1247, use gh pr edit to modify title and description.\n   - When you finalize the pull request:\n      check that pull request title and description are updated (the PR may start with a [WIP] prefix and placeholder description that should be replaced with actual title and description of the changes),\n      follow style from merged prs for code, title, and description,\n      make sure no uncommitted changes corresponding to the original requirements are left behind,\n      make sure the default branch is merged to the pull request's branch,\n      make sure all CI checks passing if they exist before you finish,\n      check for latest comments on the issue and pull request to ensure no recent feedback was missed,\n      double-check that all changes in the pull request answer to original requirements of the issue,\n      make sure no new bugs are introduced in pull request by carefully reading gh pr diff,\n      make sure no previously existing features were removed without an explicit request from users via the issue description, issue comments, and/or pull request comments.\n   - When you finish implementation, use gh pr ready 1247.\nWorkflow and collaboration.\n   - When you check branch, verify with git branch --show-current.\n   - When you push, push only to branch issue-1246-12aa15f61da1.\n   - When you finish, create a pull request from branch issue-1246-12aa15f61da1.\n   - When pr 1247 already exists for this branch, update it instead of creating new one.\n   - When you organize workflow, use pull requests instead of direct merges to default branch (main or master).\n   - When you manage commits, preserve commit history for later analysis.\n   - When you contribute, keep repository history forward-moving with regular commits, pushes, and reverts if needed.\n   - When you face conflict that you cannot resolve yourself, ask for help.\n   - When you collaborate, respect branch protections by working only on issue-1246-12aa15f61da1.\n   - When you mention result, include pull request url or comment url.\n   - When you need to create pr, remember pr 1247 already exists for this branch.\nSelf review.\n   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.\n   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.\n   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.\n   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.\n   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.\n   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.\n   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.\n   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/pulls/1247/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.\nVisual UI work and screenshots.\n   - over all other directory selection\n00236|   // This is essential for --resume to work correctly with Claude Code sessions,\n00237|   // because Claude Code stores sessions by working directory path, not session ID alone\n00238|   if (argv.workingDirectory) {\n00239|     tempDir = path.resolve(argv.workingDirectory);\n00240| \n00241|     // Check if directory exists\n00242|     try {\n00243| directory:', tempDir)}`);\n00251|           await log(formatAligned('', 'Status:', 'Using existing repository', 2));\n00252|           if (isResuming) {\n00253|             await log(formatAligned('', }\n00260|       }\n00261|     } catch {\n00262|       // Directory doesn't exist - create it\n00263|       await fs.mkdir(tempDir, { recursive: true });\n00264|       await \n00268|     return { tempDir, workspaceTmpDir, isResuming, needsClone };\n00269|   }\n00270| \n00271|   if (isResuming) {\n00272|     // When resuming without --working-directory, create a new temp \n00278|     try {\n00279|       // Check if session log exists to verify session is valid\n00280|       await fs.access(sessionLogPattern);\n00281|       await log(`🔄 Resuming session ${argv.resume} (session log found)`);\n00282| \n00283|       // For resumed sessions, create new temp directory since old one may be cleaned up\n00284|       tempDir = path.join(os.tmpdir(), ${tempDir}`);\n00287|       await log(`   Note: Claude Code sessions are tied to working directory paths.`);\n00288|       await log(`   If session resume fails, use --working-directory to specify the original directory.`);\n00289|     } catch (err) {\n00290|       reportError(err, {\n00291|         context: 'resume_session_lookup',\n00292|         sessionId: argv.resume,\n00293|         operation: // This is someone else's fork - add it as pr-fork remote\n01181|   // IMPORTANT: The fork owner's repository name is independent of our naming preferences\n01182|   // We need to discover the actual fork name, not assume it matches our convention\n01183|   // This fixes issue #1217 where incorrect fork name was used\n01184| \n01185|   await log(`${formatAligned('🔗', 'Setting up pr-fork:', }\n01203|     }\n01204|   }\n01205| \n01206|   // Strategy 2: If not found in forks list, try common naming patterns\n01207|   if (!prForkRepoName) {\n01208|     const possibleNames = [\n01209|       repo, // Standard name: \"eo2js\"\n01210|       owner ? `${owner}-${repo}` : null, // Prefixed name: \"objectionary-eo2js\"\n01211|     ].filter(Boolean);\n01212| \n01213|     await ${prForkOwner}/${candidateName} --json name 2>/dev/null`;\n01217|       if (checkResult.code === 0) {\n01218|         prForkRepoName = candidateName;\n01219|         await log(`${formatAligned('✅', These errors usually indicate you have an old version of Git, or you don't have access to the repository.\nHere's an example of an HTTPS error you might receive:\n```\n`&gt;error: The requested URL returned error: 401whileaccessing&gt;https://HOSTNAME/USER/REPO.git/info/refs?service=git-receive-pack&gt;fatal: HTTP request failed`\n```\n```\n`&gt;Error: The requested URL returned error: 403whileaccessing&gt;https://HOSTNAME/USER/REPO.git/info/refs&gt;fatal: HTTP request failed`\n```\n```\n`&gt;Error: http\n\nTitle: Troubleshooting connectivity problems - GitHub Docs\nAuthor: \nPublished Date: 2025-08-27T00:00:00.000Z\nURL: https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/using-github/troubleshooting-connectivity-problems\nText: Troubleshooting connectivity problems - GitHub Docs\n[Skip to main content] \n[GitHub Docs] \nVersion:Free, Pro, &amp; Team\nSearch or ask Copilot\nSearch or askCopilot\nSelect language: current language is English\nSearch or ask Copilot\nSearch or askCopilot\nOpen menu\nOpen Sidebar\n# Troubleshooting connectivity problems\nIf you're having trouble connecting to GitHub, you can troubleshoot your connection, then use the GitHub Debug tool to diagnose problems.\nView page as Markdown\n## In this article\nMost often, connection problems occur because a firewall, proxy server, corporate network, or other network is configured in a way that blocks GitHub.\n## [Allowing GitHub's IP addresses] \nMake sure your network is configured to allow GitHub's IP addresses. For more information, see[About GitHub's IP addresses].\n## [Using a company or organization's network] \nIf you're having connectivity problems on your company or organization's network, check with your network administrator to find out if the network has rules in place to block certain \n\nTitle: Troubleshooting authentication to a repository - GitHub Docs\nAuthor: \nURL: https://docs.github.com/en/codespaces/troubleshooting/troubleshooting-authentication-to-a-repository\nText: Troubleshooting authentication to a repository - GitHub Docs\n[Skip to main content] \n[GitHub Docs] \nVersion:Free, Pro, &amp; Team\nSearch or ask Copilot\nSearch or askCopilot\nSelect language: current language is English\nSearch or ask Copilot\nSearch or askCopilot\nOpen menu\nOpen Sidebar\n# Troubleshooting authentication to a repository\nLearn how to troubleshoot common authentication issues when you clone, push to, or pull from a repository in a codespace.\nView page as Markdown\n## In this article\nWhen you create a codespace for a repository, you can generally use`git pull`and`git push`to pull and push changes to that repository without any additional authentication. [2026-02-09T21:57:20.745Z] [INFO]         "content": "# Case Study: GitHub 500 Error During Repository Clone\n\n## Issue Overview\n- **Issue ID**: #1246  \n- **Date**: 2026-02-09\n- **Repository**: link-assistant/hive-mind\n- **External Issue**: https://github.com/bpmbpm/rdf-grapher/issues/347\n- **External Issue**: https://github.com/konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher (fork)\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nThe solve command failed with a GitHub 500 Internal Server Error when attempting to clone the repository `konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher`. This error occurred during the repository setup phase in fork mode.\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n### 2026-02-09T19:05:09.442Z - Process Start\n- solve v1.18.0 launched with command:\n```bash\n/home/hive/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.0/bin/node /home/hive/.bun/bin/solve https://github.com/bpmbpm/rdf-grapher/issues/347 --tool agent --model opencode/big-pickle --attach-logs --verbose --no-tool-check --auto-resume-on-limit-reset --tokens-budget-stats\n```\n\n### 19:05:10 - Validation Phase\n- ✅ Security warning acknowledged (--attach-logs enabled)\n- ✅ Disk space check: 56847MB available (2048MB required)\n- ✅ Memory check: 10873MB available, swap: 4095MB (0MB used), total: 14968MB (256MB required)\n- ⏩ Tool connection validation skipped (dry-run mode)\n- ⏩ GitHub authentication check skipped (dry-run mode)\n\n### 19:05:15 - Repository Access Analysis\n- ✅ Repository visibility: public (bpmbpm/rdf-grapher)\n- ✅ Auto-fork enabled: No write access detected, enabling fork mode\n- ✅ Auto-cleanup default: false (repository is public)\n- 🔍 Auto-continue check: No existing PRs found for issue #347\n- 📝 Issue mode: Working with issue #347\n\n### 19:05:18 - Fork Mode Setup\n- 🍴 Fork mode: ENABLED\n- 🔍 Detecting fork conflicts...\n- ✅ No fork conflict: Safe to proceed\n- ✅ Fork exists: konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher\n- 🔍 Validating fork parent...\n- ✅ Fork parent validated: bpmbpm/rdf-grapher\n\n### 19:05:21 - Clone Operation\n- 📥 Cloning repository: konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher\n- ⏳ 2+ minutes of clone attempt...\n- ❌ CLONE FAILED at 19:07:30\n\n### 19:07:30 - Error Analysis\n```\nremote: Internal Server Error\nfatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher.git/': The requested URL returned error: 500\nfailed to run git: exit status 128\n```\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Primary Cause: GitHub 500 Internal Server Error\n- **Nature**: Transient server-side error\n- **Location**: Git clone operation for konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher repository\n- **Timing**: After successful fork validation, during clone phase\n\n### Secondary Factors:\n1. **No Retry Mechanism**: The current implementation performs a single clone attempt\n2. **Long Clone Duration**: 2+ minutes suggests potential network/server load issues\n3. **Transient Error Pattern**: GitHub 500 errors are typically temporary infrastructure issues\n\n### Technical Analysis\n\n#### Current Clone Implementation (solve.repository.lib.mjs:888-937)\n```javascript\nexport const cloneRepository = async (repoToClone, tempDir, argv, owner, repo) => {\n  await log(`\\n${formatAligned('📥', 'Cloning repository:', repoToClone)}`);\n  \n  // Single attempt without retry logic\n  const cloneResult = await $`gh repo clone ${repoToClone} ${tempDir} 2>&1`;\n  \n  if (cloneResult.code !== 0) {\n    // Immediate failure without retry\n    await safeExit(1, 'Repository setup failed');\n  }\n};\n```\n\n#### Error Handling Gap\n- No exponential backoff retry mechanism\n- No distinction between transient (5xx) and permanent (4xx) errors\n- No alternative clone strategies (SSH fallback, different endpoints)\n\n## External Research Findings\n\n### GitHub 500 Error Patterns\nBased on research of GitHub community discussions and documentation:\n\n1. **Transient Nature**: GitHub 500 errors are typically temporary infrastructure issues\n2. **Retry Recommended**: Multiple community discussions recommend retry mechanisms\n3. **Common Scenarios**:\n   - High server load periods\n   - Git server maintenance\n   - Network infrastructure issues\n   - Temporary service degradation\n\n### Industry Best Practices\n1. **Exponential Backoff**: Implement retries with increasing delays\n2. **Error Classification**: Distinguish between transient (5xx) and permanent (4xx) errors\n3. **Multiple Strategies**: Offer alternative clone methods (SSH, different protocols)\n4. **Graceful Degradation**: Continue with alternative approaches when possible\n\n## Solution Analysis\n\n### Immediate Workarounds\n1. **Manual Retry**: User can simply run the command again\n2. **Wait Period**: Allow time for GitHub infrastructure to recover\n3. **Alternative Protocol**: Use SSH instead of HTTPS if available\n\n### Long-term Solutions\n\n#### 1. Implement Retry Mechanism\n```javascript\nexport const cloneRepositoryWithRetry = async (repoToClone, tempDir, argv, owner, repo) => {\n  const maxRetries = 3;\n  const baseDelay = 2000; // 2 seconds\n  \n  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {\n    await log(`${formatAligned('📥', 'Cloning repository:', `${repoToClone} (attempt ${attempt}/${maxRetries})`)}`);\n    \n    const cloneResult = await $`gh repo clone ${repoToClone} ${tempDir} 2>&1`;\n    \n    if (cloneResult.code === 0) {\n      await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Cloned to:', tempDir)}`);\n      return;\n    }\n    \n    const errorOutput = (cloneResult.stderr || cloneResult.stdout || '').toString();\n    \n    // Check if it's a transient error (5xx)\n    if (errorOutput.includes('error: 500') || errorOutput.includes('Internal Server Error')) {\n      if (attempt < maxRetries) {\n        const delay = baseDelay * Math.pow(2, attempt - 1); // Exponential backoff\n        await log(`${formatAligned('⏳', 'Transient error detected:', `Retrying in ${delay / 1000}s...`)}`);\n        await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, delay));\n        continue;\n      }\n    }\n    \n    // Non-transient error or max retries reached\n    await log(`${formatAligned('❌', 'CLONE FAILED', '')}`, { level: 'error' });\n    await safeExit(1, 'Repository setup failed');\n  }\n};\n```\n\n#### 2. Enhanced Error Classification\n```javascript\nconst classifyGitError = (errorOutput) => {\n  if (errorOutput.includes('error: 500') || errorOutput.includes('Internal Server Error')) {\n    return { type: 'TRANSIENT', retryable: true, description: 'GitHub server error' };\n  }\n  if (errorOutput.includes('error: 404') || errorOutput.includes('Not Found')) {\n    return { type: 'PERMANENT', retryable: false, description: 'Repository not found' };\n  }\n  if (errorOutput.includes('error: 403') || errorOutput.includes('Forbidden')) {\n    return { type: 'PERMISSION', retryable: false, description: 'Access denied' };\n  }\n  return { type: 'UNKNOWN', retryable: true, description: 'Unknown error' };\n};\n```\n\n#### 3. Alternative Clone Strategies\n```javascript\nconst tryAlternativeClone = async (repoToClone, tempDir) => {\n  // Strategy 1: Try SSH if HTTPS fails\n  const sshRepo = repoToClone.replace('https://github.com/', 'git@github.com:');\n  const sshResult = await $`git clone ${sshRepo} ${tempDir} 2>&1`;\n  \n  if (sshResult.code === 0) {\n    await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'SSH clone successful:', tempDir)}`);\n    return true;\n  }\n  \n  // Strategy 2: Try shallow clone\n  const shallowResult = await $`git clone --depth 1 ${repoToClone} ${tempDir} 2>&1`;\n  \n  if (shallowResult.code === 0) {\n    await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Shallow clone successful:', tempDir)}`);\n    return true;\n  }\n  \n  return false;\n};\n```\n\n## Impact Assessment\n\n### User Experience Impact\n- **High**: Complete failure of solve command for transient issues\n- **Frequency**: Likely low, but impact is severe when it occurs\n- **Workaround Available**: Manual retry, but poor user experience\n\n### System Reliability Impact\n- **Medium**: No graceful degradation for temporary infrastructure issues\n- **Recovery**: Requires manual intervention\n- **Automation**: Breaks automated workflows\n\n## Recommendations\n\n### Immediate Actions (High Priority)\n1. **Implement Retry Logic**: Add exponential backoff for transient 5xx errors\n2. **Error Classification**: Distinguish between retryable and non-retryable errors\n3. **User Communication**: Provide clear guidance when transient errors occur\n\n### Medium-term Improvements\n1. **Alternative Clone Methods**: Implement SSH and shallow clone fallbacks\n2. **Health Checks**: Add GitHub API health check before clone operations\n3. **Monitoring**: Track clone failure rates and patterns\n\n### Long-term Enhancements\n1. **Circuit Breaker Pattern**: Temporarily skip problematic repositories\n2. **Geographic Redundancy**: Use multiple GitHub endpoints if available\n3. **Predictive Analysis**: Use GitHub API status to anticipate issues\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n### Unit Tests\n1. **Retry Logic**: Test exponential backoff and max retry limits\n2. **Error Classification**: Verify correct error type detection\n3. **Mock Failures**: Simulate various git error scenarios\n\n### Integration Tests\n1. **Transient Failure Recovery**: Test end-to-end retry scenarios\n2. **Alternative Methods**: Verify SSH and shallow clone fallbacks\n3. **Real-world Scenarios**: Test with actual GitHub repositories\n\n### Load Testing\n1. **Concurrent Clones**: Test multiple simultaneous clone operations\n2. **Failure Injection**: Simulate GitHub API failures\n3. **Recovery Time**: Measure time-to-recovery for various scenarios\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe GitHub 500 error encountered in this case study represents a classic transient infrastructure issue that could be mitigated through proper retry mechanisms and error handling strategies. While such errors are relatively rare, their impact on user experience is severe enough to warrant implementing robust recovery mechanisms.\n\nThe proposed solutions would significantly improve the reliability of the solve command while maintaining good user experience during temporary GitHub infrastructure issues.\n\n## Related Issues and References\n\n- GitHub Community Discussion: \"Constant 500 error on Git Push\" #116700\n- GitHub Community Discussion: \"Is it recommended to retry API requests to GitHub API upon 500 response status code?\" #56013\n- Stack Overflow: \"I am getting 500 error on git clone\"\n- GitHub Docs: \"Troubleshooting cloning errors\"\n- External Issue: https://github.com/bpmbpm/rdf-grapher/issues/347 (Publisher window error: \"namedNode is not defined\")",

[2026-02-09T21:57:20.750Z] [INFO]         "content": "# Case Study: GitHub 500 Error During Repository Clone\n\n## Issue Overview\n- **Issue ID**: #1246  \n- **Date**: 2026-02-09\n- **Repository**: link-assistant/hive-mind\n- **External Issue**: https://github.com/bpmbpm/rdf-grapher/issues/347\n- **External Issue**: https://github.com/konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher (fork)\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nThe solve command failed with a GitHub 500 Internal Server Error when attempting to clone the repository `konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher`. This error occurred during the repository setup phase in fork mode.\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n### 2026-02-09T19:05:09.442Z - Process Start\n- solve v1.18.0 launched with command:\n```bash\n/home/hive/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.0/bin/node /home/hive/.bun/bin/solve https://github.com/bpmbpm/rdf-grapher/issues/347 --tool agent --model opencode/big-pickle --attach-logs --verbose --no-tool-check --auto-resume-on-limit-reset --tokens-budget-stats\n```\n\n### 19:05:10 - Validation Phase\n- ✅ Security warning acknowledged (--attach-logs enabled)\n- ✅ Disk space check: 56847MB available (2048MB required)\n- ✅ Memory check: 10873MB available, swap: 4095MB (0MB used), total: 14968MB (256MB required)\n- ⏩ Tool connection validation skipped (dry-run mode)\n- ⏩ GitHub authentication check skipped (dry-run mode)\n\n### 19:05:15 - Repository Access Analysis\n- ✅ Repository visibility: public (bpmbpm/rdf-grapher)\n- ✅ Auto-fork enabled: No write access detected, enabling fork mode\n- ✅ Auto-cleanup default: false (repository is public)\n- 🔍 Auto-continue check: No existing PRs found for issue #347\n- 📝 Issue mode: Working with issue #347\n\n### 19:05:18 - Fork Mode Setup\n- 🍴 Fork mode: ENABLED\n- 🔍 Detecting fork conflicts...\n- ✅ No fork conflict: Safe to proceed\n- ✅ Fork exists: konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher\n- 🔍 Validating fork parent...\n- ✅ Fork parent validated: bpmbpm/rdf-grapher\n\n### 19:05:21 - Clone Operation\n- 📥 Cloning repository: konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher\n- ⏳ 2+ minutes of clone attempt...\n- ❌ CLONE FAILED at 19:07:30\n\n### 19:07:30 - Error Analysis\n```\nremote: Internal Server Error\nfatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher.git/': The requested URL returned error: 500\nfailed to run git: exit status 128\n```\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Primary Cause: GitHub 500 Internal Server Error\n- **Nature**: Transient server-side error\n- **Location**: Git clone operation for konard/bpmbpm-rdf-grapher repository\n- **Timing**: After successful fork validation, during clone phase\n\n### Secondary Factors:\n1. **No Retry Mechanism**: The current implementation performs a single clone attempt\n2. **Long Clone Duration**: 2+ minutes suggests potential network/server load issues\n3. **Transient Error Pattern**: GitHub 500 errors are typically temporary infrastructure issues\n\n### Technical Analysis\n\n#### Current Clone Implementation (solve.repository.lib.mjs:888-937)\n```javascript\nexport const cloneRepository = async (repoToClone, tempDir, argv, owner, repo) => {\n  await log(`\\n${formatAligned('📥', 'Cloning repository:', repoToClone)}`);\n  \n  // Single attempt without retry logic\n  const cloneResult = await $`gh repo clone ${repoToClone} ${tempDir} 2>&1`;\n  \n  if (cloneResult.code !== 0) {\n    // Immediate failure without retry\n    await safeExit(1, 'Repository setup failed');\n  }\n};\n```\n\n#### Error Handling Gap\n- No exponential backoff retry mechanism\n- No distinction between transient (5xx) and permanent (4xx) errors\n- No alternative clone strategies (SSH fallback, different endpoints)\n\n## External Research Findings\n\n### GitHub 500 Error Patterns\nBased on research of GitHub community discussions and documentation:\n\n1. **Transient Nature**: GitHub 500 errors are typically temporary infrastructure issues\n2. **Retry Recommended**: Multiple community discussions recommend retry mechanisms\n3. **Common Scenarios**:\n   - High server load periods\n   - Git server maintenance\n   - Network infrastructure issues\n   - Temporary service degradation\n\n### Industry Best Practices\n1. **Exponential Backoff**: Implement retries with increasing delays\n2. **Error Classification**: Distinguish between transient (5xx) and permanent (4xx) errors\n3. **Multiple Strategies**: Offer alternative clone methods (SSH, different protocols)\n4. **Graceful Degradation**: Continue with alternative approaches when possible\n\n## Solution Analysis\n\n### Immediate Workarounds\n1. **Manual Retry**: User can simply run the command again\n2. **Wait Period**: Allow time for GitHub infrastructure to recover\n3. **Alternative Protocol**: Use SSH instead of HTTPS if available\n\n### Long-term Solutions\n\n#### 1. Implement Retry Mechanism\n```javascript\nexport const cloneRepositoryWithRetry = async (repoToClone, tempDir, argv, owner, repo) => {\n  const maxRetries = 3;\n  const baseDelay = 2000; // 2 seconds\n  \n  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {\n    await log(`${formatAligned('📥', 'Cloning repository:', `${repoToClone} (attempt ${attempt}/${maxRetries})`)}`);\n    \n    const cloneResult = await $`gh repo clone ${repoToClone} ${tempDir} 2>&1`;\n    \n    if (cloneResult.code === 0) {\n      await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Cloned to:', tempDir)}`);\n      return;\n    }\n    \n    const errorOutput = (cloneResult.stderr || cloneResult.stdout || '').toString();\n    \n    // Check if it's a transient error (5xx)\n    if (errorOutput.includes('error: 500') || errorOutput.includes('Internal Server Error')) {\n      if (attempt < maxRetries) {\n        const delay = baseDelay * Math.pow(2, attempt - 1); // Exponential backoff\n        await log(`${formatAligned('⏳', 'Transient error detected:', `Retrying in ${delay / 1000}s...`)}`);\n        await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, delay));\n        continue;\n      }\n    }\n    \n    // Non-transient error or max retries reached\n    await log(`${formatAligned('❌', 'CLONE FAILED', '')}`, { level: 'error' });\n    await safeExit(1, 'Repository setup failed');\n  }\n};\n```\n\n#### 2. Enhanced Error Classification\n```javascript\nconst classifyGitError = (errorOutput) => {\n  if (errorOutput.includes('error: 500') || errorOutput.includes('Internal Server Error')) {\n    return { type: 'TRANSIENT', retryable: true, description: 'GitHub server error' };\n  }\n  if (errorOutput.includes('error: 404') || errorOutput.includes('Not Found')) {\n    return { type: 'PERMANENT', retryable: false, description: 'Repository not found' };\n  }\n  if (errorOutput.includes('error: 403') || errorOutput.includes('Forbidden')) {\n    return { type: 'PERMISSION', retryable: false, description: 'Access denied' };\n  }\n  return { type: 'UNKNOWN', retryable: true, description: 'Unknown error' };\n};\n```\n\n#### 3. Alternative Clone Strategies\n```javascript\nconst tryAlternativeClone = async (repoToClone, tempDir) => {\n  // Strategy 1: Try SSH if HTTPS fails\n  const sshRepo = repoToClone.replace('https://github.com/', 'git@github.com:');\n  const sshResult = await $`git clone ${sshRepo} ${tempDir} 2>&1`;\n  \n  if (sshResult.code === 0) {\n    await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'SSH clone successful:', tempDir)}`);\n    return true;\n  }\n  \n  // Strategy 2: Try shallow clone\n  const shallowResult = await $`git clone --depth 1 ${repoToClone} ${tempDir} 2>&1`;\n  \n  if (shallowResult.code === 0) {\n    await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Shallow clone successful:', tempDir)}`);\n    return true;\n  }\n  \n  return false;\n};\n```\n\n## Impact Assessment\n\n### User Experience Impact\n- **High**: Complete failure of solve command for transient issues\n- **Frequency**: Likely low, but impact is severe when it occurs\n- **Workaround Available**: Manual retry, but poor user experience\n\n### System Reliability Impact\n- **Medium**: No graceful degradation for temporary infrastructure issues\n- **Recovery**: Requires manual intervention\n- **Automation**: Breaks automated workflows\n\n## Recommendations\n\n### Immediate Actions (High Priority)\n1. **Implement Retry Logic**: Add exponential backoff for transient 5xx errors\n2. **Error Classification**: Distinguish between retryable and non-retryable errors\n3. **User Communication**: Provide clear guidance when transient errors occur\n\n### Medium-term Improvements\n1. **Alternative Clone Methods**: Implement SSH and shallow clone fallbacks\n2. **Health Checks**: Add GitHub API health check before clone operations\n3. **Monitoring**: Track clone failure rates and patterns\n\n### Long-term Enhancements\n1. **Circuit Breaker Pattern**: Temporarily skip problematic repositories\n2. **Geographic Redundancy**: Use multiple GitHub endpoints if available\n3. **Predictive Analysis**: Use GitHub API status to anticipate issues\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n### Unit Tests\n1. **Retry Logic**: Test exponential backoff and max retry limits\n2. **Error Classification**: Verify correct error type detection\n3. **Mock Failures**: Simulate various git error scenarios\n\n### Integration Tests\n1. **Transient Failure Recovery**: Test end-to-end retry scenarios\n2. **Alternative Methods**: Verify SSH and shallow clone fallbacks\n3. **Real-world Scenarios**: Test with actual GitHub repositories\n\n### Load Testing\n1. **Concurrent Clones**: Test multiple simultaneous clone operations\n2. **Failure Injection**: Simulate GitHub API failures\n3. **Recovery Time**: Measure time-to-recovery for various scenarios\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe GitHub 500 error encountered in this case study represents a classic transient infrastructure issue that could be mitigated through proper retry mechanisms and error handling strategies. While such errors are relatively rare, their impact on user experience is severe enough to warrant implementing robust recovery mechanisms.\n\nThe proposed solutions would significantly improve the reliability of the solve command while maintaining good user experience during temporary GitHub infrastructure issues.\n\n## Related Issues and References\n\n- GitHub Community Discussion: \"Constant 500 error on Git Push\" #116700\n- GitHub Community Discussion: \"Is it recommended to retry API requests to GitHub API upon 500 response status code?\" #56013\n- Stack Overflow: \"I am getting 500 error on git clone\"\n- GitHub Docs: \"Troubleshooting cloning errors\"\n- External Issue: https://github.com/bpmbpm/rdf-grapher/issues/347 (Publisher window error: \"namedNode is not defined\")",

Reads RDFa and JSON-LD\n- Read/Write Linked Data client, using WebDav or SPARQL/Update\n- Real-Time Collaborative editing with web sockets and PATCHes\n- Local API for querying a store\n- Compatible with [RDFJS task force spec] \n- SPARQL queries (not full SPARQL - just graph match and optional)\n- Smushing of nodes from `owl:sameAs`, and `owl:{f,inverseF}unctionProperty`\n- Tracks provenance of triples keeps metadata (in RDF) from HTTP accesses\n\n## Documentation\n\nSee:\n\n- The [API documentation] is partial but useful\n\n- [Tutorial: Using rdflib in a Solid web app] \n\n- [Tutorial: Using rdflib.js] \n\n- [Tutorial: Using Turtle] \n\n- [Using authenticated & alternate fetch methods] \n\n- [Block diagram: rdflib modules] \n\n- [Block diagram\n\nTitle: @tpluscode/rdf-ns-builders\nAuthor: \nPublished Date: 2025-03-26T00:00:00.000Z\nURL: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tpluscode/rdf-ns-builders\nText: ## @tpluscode/rdf-ns-builders ![TypeScript icon, indicating that this package has built-in type declarations] \n\n5.0.0 • Public • Published 2 months ago\n\n- [Readme] \n- [Code Beta] \n- [5 Dependencies] \n- [66 Dependents] \n- [37 Versions] \n\n> # @tpluscode/rdf-ns-builders\n\nCommon RDF vocabularies extracted from [`@zazuko/vocabularies`] and wrapped\nas [`@rdfjs/namespace`] builder objects.\n\nAlso includes TypeScript declarations of the terms included in the known\nvocabularies as extracted from the actual RDF source file.\n\n## Why?\n\n1. If you're tired of typing `const schema = namespace(prefixes.schema)` every so often\n2. If you want to have your IDE \"Find usages\" of a `@rdfjs/namespace`-generated term\n\n## Installation\n\n```\nnpm i -S @tpluscode/rdf-ns-builders\n\n```\n\n## Usage\n\nSimply import a namespace builder by its prefix defined in [`@zazuko/vocabularies`] \n\n```\nimport { schema } from '@tpluscode/rdf-ns-builders'\nimport { NamedNode } from '@rdfjs/types'\n\nconst schemaPerson: NamedNode = schema.Person\n```\n\n### Usage with RDF/JS Environment\n\nYou can also add all vocabularies to an environmen\n\nTitle: Comunica – RDF/JS\nAuthor: \nPublished Date: 2024-07-05T00:00:00.000Z\nURL: https://comunica.dev/docs/query/advanced/rdfjs\nText: Comunica –\\ RDF/JS\n* [Documentation] \n* [Query with Comunica] \n* [Advanced querying] \n* RDF/JS# RDF/JS\nOn this page\n[![RDF/JS logo]] \nRDF/JS offers a set of RDF specifications for JavaScript libraries\nthat are defined by the[RDF JavaScript Libraries W3C community group].\nMost of the popular JavaScript libraries adhere to these specifications, which makes it possible to use them interchangeably, and in any combination.\nThis allows you to for example use an RDF parser from one developer, and pipe its output into an RDF store from another developer.\nFor most of these specifications, corresponding[TypeScript typings exist],\nand many libraries ship with their own typings as well,\nwhich makes RDF/JS especially useful if you want to develop more strongly-typed JavaScript applications.\nComunica is conformant to the following RDF/JS specifications.\n## Data model specification\nThe foundational part of RDF/JS is its[low-level**data model**specification],\nin which JavaScript interfaces are described for representing**RDF terms**and**RDF quads**.\nFive types of terms exist:\n* [Named Node]: Represents a thing by \n\nTitle: @rdfjs/namespace\nAuthor: \nPublished Date: 2024-02-09T00:00:00.000Z\nURL: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@rdfjs/namespace\nText: # @rdfjs/namespace [![DefinitelyTyped icon, indicating that this package has TypeScript declarations provided by the separate @types/rdfjs__namespace package]] \n\n2.0.1 • Public • Published 4 months ago\n\n- [Readme] \n- [Code Beta] \n- [1 Dependency] \n- [81 Dependents] \n- [5 Versions] \n\n# @rdfjs/namespace\n\n[![build status]] [![npm version]] \n\n[Named Node] builder.\n\n## Usage\n\nThe package exports a factory to create builders for [Named Nodes].\nThe `baseIRI` given to the factory will be used together with the parameter given to the builder to create Named Nodes.\n\n### Factory\n\nThe factory must be called with the `baseIRI` as a string:\n\n```\nconst builder = namespace('http://example.org/base-iri#')\n```\n\nAn optional factory can be defined:\n\n```\nconst builder = namespace('http://example.org/base-iri#', { factory: customFactory })\n```\n\nBy default `@rdfjs/data-model` is used.\n\n### Builder\n\nThe builder can be called in three different ways:\n\n- function call: ( `builder('property')`)\n- tagged template string: ( ``builder`property` ``)\n- property: ( `builder.property`)\n\nCalling the builder using the propery requires\n\nTitle: Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\nAuthor: [2026-02-09T21:58:03.508Z] [INFO]         "oldString": "// Clone repository and set up remotes\nexport const cloneRepository = async (repoToClone, tempDir, argv, owner, repo) => {\n  // Clone the repository (or fork) using gh tool with authentication\n  await log(`\\n${formatAligned('📥', 'Cloning repository:', repoToClone)}`);\n\n  // Use 2>&1 to capture all output and filter \"Cloning into\" message\n  const cloneResult = await $`gh repo clone ${repoToClone} ${tempDir} 2>&1`;\n\n  // Verify clone was successful\n  if (cloneResult.code !== 0) {\n    const errorOutput = (cloneResult.stderr || cloneResult.stdout || 'Unknown error').toString().trim();\n    await log('');\n    await log(`${formatAligned('❌', 'CLONE FAILED', '')}`, { level: 'error' });\n    await log('');\n    await log('  🔍 What happened:');\n    await log(`     Failed to clone repository ${repoToClone}`);\n    await log('');\n    await log('  📦 Error details:');\n    for (const line of errorOutput.split('\\n')) {\n      if (line.trim()) await log(`     ${line}`);\n    }\n    await log('');\n    await log('  💡 Common causes:');\n    await log(\"     • Repository doesn't exist or is private\");\n    await log('     • No GitHub authentication');\n    await log('     • Network connectivity issues');\n    if (argv.fork) {\n      await log('     • Fork not ready yet (try again in a moment)');\n    }\n    await log('');\n    await log('  🔧 How to fix:');\n    await log('     1. Check authentication: gh auth status');\n    await log('     2. Login if needed: gh auth login');\n    await log(`     3. Verify access: gh repo view ${owner}/${repo}`);\n    if (argv.fork) {\n      await log(`     4. Check fork: gh repo view ${repoToClone}`);\n    }\n    await log('');\n    await safeExit(1, 'Repository setup failed');\n  }\n\n  await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Cloned to:', tempDir)}`);\n\n  // Verify and fix remote configuration\n  const remoteCheckResult = await $({ cwd: tempDir })`git remote -v 2>&1`;\n  if (!remoteCheckResult.stdout || !remoteCheckResult.stdout.toString().includes('origin')) {\n    await log('   Setting up git remote...', { verbose: true });\n    // Add origin remote manually\n    await $({ cwd: tempDir })`git remote add origin https://github.com/${repoToClone}.git 2>&1`;\n  }\n};",
[2026-02-09T21:58:03.508Z] [INFO]         "newString": "// Classify git clone errors to determine if they are retryable\nconst classifyCloneError = (errorOutput) => {\n  const output = errorOutput.toLowerCase();\n  \n  // Transient server errors (5xx) - typically retryable\n  if (output.includes('error: 500') || output.includes('internal server error') || \n      output.includes('error: 502') || output.includes('error: 503') || output.includes('error: 504')) {\n    return { type: 'TRANSIENT', retryable: true, description: 'GitHub server error' };\n  }\n  \n  // Network-related errors - typically retryable\n  if (output.includes('connection refused') || output.includes('connection timed out') || \n      output.includes('connection reset') || output.includes('unable to connect') ||\n      output.includes('network is unreachable') || output.includes('ssl error')) {\n    return { type: 'NETWORK', retryable: true, description: 'Network connectivity issue' };\n  }\n  \n  // Authentication/permission errors - not retryable\n  if (output.includes('error: 401') || output.includes('error: 403') || \n      output.includes('authentication failed') || output.includes('permission denied')) {\n    return { type: 'PERMISSION', retryable: false, description: 'Authentication or permission error' };\n  }\n  \n  // Repository not found - not retryable\n  if (output.includes('error: 404') || output.includes('not found') || \n      output.includes('repository not found')) {\n    return { type: 'NOT_FOUND', retryable: false, description: 'Repository not found' };\n  }\n  \n  // Rate limiting - retryable with backoff\n  if (output.includes('rate limit') || output.includes('too many requests') || \n      output.includes('api rate limit exceeded')) {\n    return { type: 'RATE_LIMIT', retryable: true, description: 'Rate limit exceeded' };\n  }\n  \n  // Default to retryable for unknown errors\n  return { type: 'UNKNOWN', retryable: true, description: 'Unknown error' };\n};\n\n// Clone repository and set up remotes with retry mechanism\nexport const cloneRepository = async (repoToClone, tempDir, argv, owner, repo) => {\n  const maxRetries = 3;\n  const baseDelay = 2000; // Start with 2 seconds\n  let lastError = null;\n\n  await log(`\\n${formatAligned('📥', 'Cloning repository:', repoToClone)}`);\n\n  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {\n    if (attempt > 1) {\n      await log(`${formatAligned('⏳', 'Clone attempt:', `${attempt}/${maxRetries} (with retry logic)`)}`);\n    }\n\n    // Use 2>&1 to capture all output and filter \"Cloning into\" message\n    const cloneResult = await $`gh repo clone ${repoToClone} ${tempDir} 2>&1`;\n\n    // Verify clone was successful\n    if (cloneResult.code === 0) {\n      await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Cloned to:', tempDir)}`);\n      \n      // Verify and fix remote configuration\n      const remoteCheckResult = await $({ cwd: tempDir })`git remote -v 2>&1`;\n      if (!remoteCheckResult.stdout || !remoteCheckResult.stdout.toString().includes('origin')) {\n        await log('   Setting up git remote...', { verbose: true });\n        // Add origin remote manually\n        await $({ cwd: tempDir })`git remote add origin https://github.com/${repoToClone}.git 2>&1`;\n      }\n      return; // Success - exit function\n    }\n\n    // Clone failed - analyze error and determine if retry is appropriate\n    const errorOutput = (cloneResult.stderr || cloneResult.stdout || 'Unknown error').toString().trim();\n    lastError = errorOutput;\n    \n    const errorClassification = classifyCloneError(errorOutput);\n    \n    if (!errorClassification.retryable || attempt === maxRetries) {\n      // Non-retryable error or max retries reached - fail with detailed error\n      await log('');\n      await log(`${formatAligned('❌', 'CLONE FAILED', '')}`, { level: 'error' });\n      await log('');\n      await log('  🔍 What happened:');\n      await log(`     Failed to clone repository ${repoToClone}`);\n      \n      if (!errorClassification.retryable) {\n        await log(`     Error type: ${errorClassification.description} (not retryable)`);\n      } else {\n        await log(`     Error type: ${errorClassification.description} (max retries exceeded)`);\n      }\n      await log('');\n      await log('  📦 Error details:');\n      for (const line of errorOutput.split('\\n')) {\n        if (line.trim()) await log(`     ${line}`);\n      }\n      await log('');\n      await log('  💡 Common causes:');\n      await log(\"     • Repository doesn't exist or is private\");\n      await log('     • No GitHub authentication');\n      await log('     • Network connectivity issues');\n      if (errorClassification.type === 'TRANSIENT') {\n        await log('     • GitHub server issues (temporary)');\n      }\n      if (errorClassification.type === 'RATE_LIMIT') {\n        await log('     • API rate limiting exceeded');\n      }\n      if (argv.fork) {\n        await log('     • Fork not ready yet (try again in a moment)');\n      }\n      await log('');\n      await log('  🔧 How to fix:');\n      await log('     1. Check authentication: gh auth status');\n      await log('     2. Login if needed: gh auth login');\n      await log(`     3. Verify access: gh repo view ${owner}/${repo}`);\n      if (argv.fork) {\n        await log(`     4. Check fork: gh repo view ${repoToClone}`);\n      }\n      if (errorClassification.type === 'TRANSIENT') {\n        await log('     5. Wait a few minutes and retry (GitHub server issue)');\n        await log('     6. Check GitHub status: https://www.githubstatus.com');\n      }\n      if (errorClassification.type === 'RATE_LIMIT') {\n        await log('     5. Wait for rate limit to reset (check your quota)');\n        await log('     6. Use --token flag with different token if available');\n      }\n      await log('');\n      await safeExit(1, 'Repository setup failed');\n    }\n\n    // Retryable error and we have attempts left\n    const delay = baseDelay * Math.pow(2, attempt - 1); // Exponential backoff\n    await log(`${formatAligned('⚠️', 'Clone failed:', errorClassification.description)}`);\n    await log(`${formatAligned('⏳', 'Retrying:', `Waiting ${delay / 1000}s before attempt ${attempt + 1}/${maxRetries}...`)}`);\n    \n    if (errorClassification.type === 'RATE_LIMIT') {\n      await log('     💡 Tip: Rate limiting detected - using longer delay');\n    }\n    \n    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, delay));\n  }\n\n  // This should never be reached due to the loop logic above\n  await log(`${formatAligned('❌', 'UNEXPECTED ERROR:', 'Clone logic failed')}`);\n  await safeExit(1, 'Repository setup failed');\n};"

[2026-02-09T21:58:03.538Z] [INFO]         "newString": "// Classify git clone errors to determine if they are retryable\nconst classifyCloneError = (errorOutput) => {\n  const output = errorOutput.toLowerCase();\n  \n  // Transient server errors (5xx) - typically retryable\n  if (output.includes('error: 500') || output.includes('internal server error') || \n      output.includes('error: 502') || output.includes('error: 503') || output.includes('error: 504')) {\n    return { type: 'TRANSIENT', retryable: true, description: 'GitHub server error' };\n  }\n  \n  // Network-related errors - typically retryable\n  if (output.includes('connection refused') || output.includes('connection timed out') || \n      output.includes('connection reset') || output.includes('unable to connect') ||\n      output.includes('network is unreachable') || output.includes('ssl error')) {\n    return { type: 'NETWORK', retryable: true, description: 'Network connectivity issue' };\n  }\n  \n  // Authentication/permission errors - not retryable\n  if (output.includes('error: 401') || output.includes('error: 403') || \n      output.includes('authentication failed') || output.includes('permission denied')) {\n    return { type: 'PERMISSION', retryable: false, description: 'Authentication or permission error' };\n  }\n  \n  // Repository not found - not retryable\n  if (output.includes('error: 404') || output.includes('not found') || \n      output.includes('repository not found')) {\n    return { type: 'NOT_FOUND', retryable: false, description: 'Repository not found' };\n  }\n  \n  // Rate limiting - retryable with backoff\n  if (output.includes('rate limit') || output.includes('too many requests') || \n      output.includes('api rate limit exceeded')) {\n    return { type: 'RATE_LIMIT', retryable: true, description: 'Rate limit exceeded' };\n  }\n  \n  // Default to retryable for unknown errors\n  return { type: 'UNKNOWN', retryable: true, description: 'Unknown error' };\n};\n\n// Clone repository and set up remotes with retry mechanism\nexport const cloneRepository = async (repoToClone, tempDir, argv, owner, repo) => {\n  const maxRetries = 3;\n  const baseDelay = 2000; // Start with 2 seconds\n  let lastError = null;\n\n  await log(`\\n${formatAligned('📥', 'Cloning repository:', repoToClone)}`);\n\n  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {\n    if (attempt > 1) {\n      await log(`${formatAligned('⏳', 'Clone attempt:', `${attempt}/${maxRetries} (with retry logic)`)}`);\n    }\n\n    // Use 2>&1 to capture all output and filter \"Cloning into\" message\n    const cloneResult = await $`gh repo clone ${repoToClone} ${tempDir} 2>&1`;\n\n    // Verify clone was successful\n    if (cloneResult.code === 0) {\n      await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Cloned to:', tempDir)}`);\n      \n      // Verify and fix remote configuration\n      const remoteCheckResult = await $({ cwd: tempDir })`git remote -v 2>&1`;\n      if (!remoteCheckResult.stdout || !remoteCheckResult.stdout.toString().includes('origin')) {\n        await log('   Setting up git remote...', { verbose: true });\n        // Add origin remote manually\n        await $({ cwd: tempDir })`git remote add origin https://github.com/${repoToClone}.git 2>&1`;\n      }\n      return; // Success - exit function\n    }\n\n    // Clone failed - analyze error and determine if retry is appropriate\n    const errorOutput = (cloneResult.stderr || cloneResult.stdout || 'Unknown error').toString().trim();\n    lastError = errorOutput;\n    \n    const errorClassification = classifyCloneError(errorOutput);\n    \n    if (!errorClassification.retryable || attempt === maxRetries) {\n      // Non-retryable error or max retries reached - fail with detailed error\n      await log('');\n      await log(`${formatAligned('❌', 'CLONE FAILED', '')}`, { level: 'error' });\n      await log('');\n      await log('  🔍 What happened:');\n      await log(`     Failed to clone repository ${repoToClone}`);\n      \n      if (!errorClassification.retryable) {\n        await log(`     Error type: ${errorClassification.description} (not retryable)`);\n      } else {\n        await log(`     Error type: ${errorClassification.description} (max retries exceeded)`);\n      }\n      await log('');\n      await log('  📦 Error details:');\n      for (const line of errorOutput.split('\\n')) {\n        if (line.trim()) await log(`     ${line}`);\n      }\n      await log('');\n      await log('  💡 Common causes:');\n      await log(\"     • Repository doesn't exist or is private\");\n      await log('     • No GitHub authentication');\n      await log('     • Network connectivity issues');\n      if (errorClassification.type === 'TRANSIENT') {\n        await log('     • GitHub server issues (temporary)');\n      }\n      if (errorClassification.type === 'RATE_LIMIT') {\n        await log('     • API rate limiting exceeded');\n      }\n      if (argv.fork) {\n        await log('     • Fork not ready yet (try again in a moment)');\n      }\n      await log('');\n      await log('  🔧 How to fix:');\n      await log('     1. Check authentication: gh auth status');\n      await log('     2. Login if needed: gh auth login');\n      await log(`     3. Verify access: gh repo view ${owner}/${repo}`);\n      if (argv.fork) {\n        await log(`     4. Check fork: gh repo view ${repoToClone}`);\n      }\n      if (errorClassification.type === 'TRANSIENT') {\n        await log('     5. Wait a few minutes and retry (GitHub server issue)');\n        await log('     6. Check GitHub status: https://www.githubstatus.com');\n      }\n      if (errorClassification.type === 'RATE_LIMIT') {\n        await log('     5. Wait for rate limit to reset (check your quota)');\n        await log('     6. Use --token flag with different token if available');\n      }\n      await log('');\n      await safeExit(1, 'Repository setup failed');\n    }\n\n    // Retryable error and we have attempts left\n    const delay = baseDelay * Math.pow(2, attempt - 1); // Exponential backoff\n    await log(`${formatAligned('⚠️', 'Clone failed:', errorClassification.description)}`);\n    await log(`${formatAligned('⏳', 'Retrying:', `Waiting ${delay / 1000}s before attempt ${attempt + 1}/${maxRetries}...`)}`);\n    \n    if (errorClassification.type === 'RATE_LIMIT') {\n      await log('     💡 Tip: Rate limiting detected - using longer delay');\n    }\n    \n    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, delay));\n  }\n\n  // This should never be reached due to the loop logic above\n  await log(`${formatAligned('❌', 'UNEXPECTED ERROR:', 'Clone logic failed')}`);\n  await safeExit(1, 'Repository setup failed');\n};"
